word_list.word: anger is the one thing made better by delay
word_list.pronunciation: ˈæŋɡɚɹ ɪz ðə wˈʌn θˈɪŋ mˌeɪd bˈɛɾɚ baɪ dɪlˈeɪ

senses.definition: used to highlight the importance of controlling emotions and suggests that delaying a response to situations that cause anger can lead to a more appropriate reaction

word_list.word: let not the sun go down on your (wrath|anger)
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɛt nˌɑːt ðə sˈʌn ɡˌoʊ dˌaʊn ˌɑːn jʊɹ ɹˈæθ ˈæŋɡɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that one should not hold onto feelings of anger or resentment for too long and should strive to resolve conflicts or issues before the end of the day

examples.x: "I know we had a disagreement earlier, but let not the sun go down on your wrath," said Sarah, trying to reconcile with her sister.

word_list.word: it is not work that kills, but worry
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz nˌɑːt wˈɜːk ðæt kˈɪlz bˌʌt wˈʌɹi

senses.definition: used to suggest that stress and anxiety can be more harmful to one's health and well-being than hard physical labor

word_list.word: you can only die once, so do not die a thousand times worrying about it
word_list.pronunciation: juː kæn ˈoʊnli dˈaɪ wˈʌns sˌoʊ duːnˌɑːt dˈaɪ ɐ θˈaʊzənd tˈaɪmz wˈʌɹɪɪŋ ɐbˈaʊt ɪt

senses.definition: used to suggest that excessive worry and anxiety can be more harmful than the actual event that is causing the worry

word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛnvi hɐz nˈɛvɚɹ ɛnɹˈɪtʃt ˌɛni mˈæn

senses.definition: used t suggest that being envious of others does not lead to personal success or enrichment, emphasizing the importance of focusing on one's own strengths and accomplishments

examples.x: The athlete learned that envy never enriched any man, so he focused on his own training and improvement instead of being jealous of his competitors.

word_list.word: envy shoots at others and wounds (itself|herself)
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛnvi ʃˈuːts æt ˈʌðɚz ænd wˈuːndz ɪtsˈɛlf hɜːsˈɛlf

senses.definition: used to suggest that envy can harm oneself more than the person one is envious of, emphasizing the negative impact of envy on personal well-being and success

examples.x: The envious politician attacked his opponent with baseless accusations, not realizing that envy shoots at others and wounds itself, as his own reputation was tarnished in the process.

senses.definition: used to highlight the negative impact of jealousy on personal relationships, emphasizing its potential to breed resentment, suspicion, and destructive behavior

word_list.word: age may wrinkle the face, but lack of enthusiasm wrinkles the soul
word_list.pronunciation: ˈeɪdʒ mˈeɪ ɹˈɪŋkəl ðə fˈeɪs bˌʌt lˈæk ʌv ɛnθˈuːzɪˌæzəm ɹˈɪŋkəlz ðə sˈoʊl

senses.definition: used to imply that while aging may cause physical changes, a lack of passion or enthusiasm can have a more profound effect on one's inner self and sense of fulfillment

word_list.word: it is a (poor|sad) heart that never rejoices
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ɐ pˈʊɹ sˈæd hˈɑːɹt ðæt nˈɛvɚ ɹɪdʒˈɔɪsᵻz

senses.definition: used to imply that joy can be found even in difficult circumstances, and that a life without joy or happiness is lacking in richness and fulfillment

examples.x: Despite living in poverty, the family found happiness in their love for one another and the simple pleasures of life, understanding that it is a poor heart that never rejoices.

word_list.word: all things grow with time except grief
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɔːl θˈɪŋz ɡɹˈoʊ wɪð tˈaɪm ɛksˈɛpt ɡɹˈiːf

senses.definition: used to suggest that with the passage of time, the intensity of grief gradually decreases

examples.x: Mark's friend had passed away, and he found that all things grow with time except grief, as he slowly learned to cope with the loss and cherish the memories they shared.

word_list.word: a guilty conscience needs no accuser
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ ɡˈɪlti kˈɑːnʃəns nˈiːdz nˈoʊ ɐkjˈuːzɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that feelings of guilt or unease can be a powerful motivator for someone to confess or seek forgiveness, even if no one else is aware of their wrongdoing

examples.x: Mark had been involved in a wrongdoing at work, but as they say, a guilty conscience needs no accuser, and he eventually came forward to his boss and admitted his mistake.

word_list.word: nice work if you can get it
word_list.pronunciation: nˈaɪs wˈɜːk ɪf juː kæn ɡˈɛt ɪt

senses.definition: used for expressing jealousy over what someone already has or achieved without putting much effort

examples.x: She married a millionaire and now she doesn't have to work - nice work if you can get it.
